skill.name: woocommerce-dev-cycle
skill.description: Guide WooCommerce developers through test runs, linting, code quality checks, and common formatting fixes for the WooCommerce repository.
skill.summary: Run focused WooCommerce tests, lint changed files, and fix common PHP and markdown quality issues.
skill.author: woocommerce

security_audit.summary: The static command findings are mostly documentation examples for normal WooCommerce development commands, not hidden executable code. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or malicious network behavior was found. The skill still warrants a medium warning because it directs agents to run package installation, Docker test environment, lint fix, git, and file-moving commands that can modify local state.

medium_findings.title: Agent-Directed Development Commands Can Modify Local State
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE for operational risk, but not malicious. The skill instructs agents to run lint fixers, git commands, wp-env lifecycle commands, and file-moving shell commands. These are legitimate WooCommerce workflows, but they can alter repository files or local Docker test environments. Confidence: 0.86. Confidence reasoning: The commands are directly present in documentation and their state-changing behavior is clear, while there is no evidence of hostile intent.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly present in documentation and their state-changing behavior is clear. The surrounding context is normal development workflow guidance, not malware.

medium_findings.title: Package Installation Commands May Use the Network
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE for network and supply-chain exposure. The skill recommends npm or pnpm installation when tooling is missing, which can download dependencies from package registries. This is expected developer setup behavior, but marketplace users should approve such commands before execution. Confidence: 0.80. Confidence reasoning: The install commands are explicit, but they do not include suspicious domains, secret handling, or exfiltration logic.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The install commands are explicit and normally require network access. No suspicious destination, credential collection, or data upload pattern was found.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The high-severity weak cryptography alerts correspond to markdown rule codes, sample output, or ordinary documentation text, not MD5, SHA1, DES, or other weak cryptographic operations. No cryptographic API use was found in the reviewed skill files. Confidence: 0.94. Confidence reasoning: The cited files are markdown guidance, and the reviewed lines do not contain executable cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited files are markdown guidance, and the reviewed lines do not contain executable cryptographic code. The detector appears to have matched short text tokens rather than semantics.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Evidence Found
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for prompt injection concerns. Review found no text telling the agent to ignore higher-priority instructions, override security analysis, claim pre-approval, or assume elevated authority. Confidence: 0.90. Confidence reasoning: Targeted review covered all skill files and found ordinary workflow guidance only.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review covered the skill instructions and referenced markdown files. The wording is strict development guidance, not an attempt to override marketplace or system instructions.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Commands Embedded in Skill Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains many shell command examples for pnpm, npm, wp-env, git, markdownlint, phpcs, and file utilities. These should be treated as commands requiring normal user approval and project-context checks. Confidence: 0.88. Confidence reasoning: The pattern is pervasive and intentional, but the commands support the stated WooCommerce development purpose.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell examples are visible across the documentation. Their purpose is development workflow automation, not concealment or unauthorized execution.

content.user_title: Run WooCommerce Tests and Quality Checks
content.value_statement: WooCommerce development requires the right test and lint commands for each change. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through focused tests, formatting checks, and common fixes.
actual_capabilities: Selects WooCommerce PHP and JavaScript test commands for changed code.
Explains branch-focused PHP, JavaScript, and markdown linting workflows.
Lists common PHPCS formatting issues and preferred WooCommerce fixes.
Guides markdownlint setup, auto-fix usage, and encoding checks.
Helps interpret PHPUnit output and common test environment failures.
Provides pre-commit quality checks for WooCommerce repository changes.
limitations: Does not run tests or linting without tool access and user approval.
Assumes the WooCommerce repository uses the documented pnpm and wp-env scripts.
Does not replace human review of automatic lint fixes or git changes.
Does not provide general WooCommerce feature architecture guidance.

use_cases.title: Validate a WooCommerce PHP Change
use_cases.description: Run focused PHPUnit tests and branch-specific PHP linting before opening a pull request.

use_cases.title: Fix Documentation Formatting
use_cases.description: Apply markdownlint guidance, add language tags, and verify clean markdown files.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Safe Commit
use_cases.description: Review changed files, run relevant checks, and avoid unrelated formatting churn.

prompt_templates.title: Run Basic Checks
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for a small WooCommerce change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the WooCommerce development cycle skill. Identify the focused tests and lint commands I should run for my current changed files.

prompt_templates.title: Fix PHP Lint Issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the WooCommerce development cycle skill. Review my PHPCS output, explain each issue, and suggest fixes only for files changed in this branch.

prompt_templates.title: Repair Markdown Formatting
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the WooCommerce development cycle skill. Help me fix markdownlint errors, preserve UTF-8 directory trees, and verify the file after auto-fix.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Full Pre-Commit Pass
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow before a pull request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the WooCommerce development cycle skill. Build a pre-commit verification plan for my PHP, JavaScript, and markdown changes, including targeted tests and review steps.

output_examples.input: I changed a WooCommerce PHP payment settings class and one markdown guide.
output: Run the PHP tests that cover the changed payment settings behavior.
Run branch-specific PHP linting and review any automatic fixes.
Run markdownlint on the changed guide and verify encoding if directory trees are present.

output_examples.input: PHPCS says my translator comment is in the wrong place.
output_examples.output: Move the translator comment directly before the translation function call. Keep the change limited to the affected file and rerun the specific PHP lint command.

output_examples.input: My JavaScript tests are not being found in client/admin.
output_examples.output: Confirm you are in the client/admin directory, install dependencies if needed, and run the focused JavaScript test command for the changed test file.

best_practices: Run focused tests first, then broaden checks before committing.
Review every automatic lint fix with git diff before keeping it.
Limit lint cleanup to files changed in the current branch.
anti_patterns: Running whole-repository lint fixes for a small branch change.
Using absolute markdownlint paths that bypass repository configuration.
Committing after auto-fix commands without reviewing the resulting diff.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides command guidance. The assistant still needs tool access and user approval to run commands.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The commands are legitimate, but some can install packages, modify files, reset files, or change a local Docker test environment.

faq.question: Can it help with PHP linting errors?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers common WooCommerce PHPCS issues such as translator comments, docblock order, indentation, and unused parameters.

faq.question: Can it help with JavaScript tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes the client/admin test workflow and common troubleshooting steps for JavaScript tests.

faq.question: Does it cover markdown formatting?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents markdownlint commands, common rules, language tags, line breaks, and encoding checks.

faq.question: Is it only for WooCommerce?
faq.answer: Yes. The commands and conventions are specific to the WooCommerce repository and its development scripts.
